page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-80 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the N2 family. It features 80 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz, configured with 40 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 80.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 75,231.71 and a PassMark CPU score of 52,249.76, both placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is also a key strength, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 4,123.03 GB/sec and large block uncached read speeds at 205.69 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is moderate, as evidenced by average ratings in Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core tests.

The primary tradeoff of the n2-highcpu-80 is its low memory density of 1 GB per vCPU, which limits its suitability for memory-bound workloads. Additionally, single-threaded operations and text generation tasks, such as LLM text generation, perform in the bottom 25%. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers high compute density and strong multi-threaded throughput relative to its hardware profile, making it a cost-effective choice for CPU-bound parallel processing that does not require massive memory footprints or local storage.

This instance is highly suited for multi-threaded compute workloads, including software compilation, database operations, Redis caching layers, and parallelized data processing. It is also appropriate for static web serving and prompt processing phases of language model inference, though it is less optimal for memory-intensive databases or active text generation.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-80 is an x86_64 compute-focused server instance featuring 80 dedicated vCPUs and 80.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz across 40 physical cores, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. It delivers strong multi-core performance, as demonstrated by a stress-ng multi-core score of 75,231.71 and high memory bandwidth, though single-core performance and text generation speeds remain average to weak. The low 1 GB per vCPU memory ratio represents a clear tradeoff, making it less suitable for memory-intensive applications. Qualitatively, the instance provides high compute density and resource efficiency for parallel processing. It is best suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-80 is an x86_64 server with 80 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 80 GB RAM. It offers strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-80 features 80 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 80 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density, multi-core compute instance optimized for parallel processing, database operations, and high-bandwidth memory workloads without local storage.

Strong multi-core CPU and high-bandwidth memory performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Low memory-to-core ratio of 1.0 GB per vCPU
Best suited for software compilation, database operations, and Redis caching
